Scenario.title: Juicy Mix Attack Simulation
Scenario.overview: Energy Sector Simple This simulated attack demonstrates a straightforward compromise targeting energy sector organizations. The threat group 'Juicy Mix' employs spearphishing via internet-facing assets to deliver a Mango backdoor payload, achieving persistence through file manipulation methods.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Endpoint, Network Infrastructure, User Workstations
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Social Engineering, Web-based Spearphishing, Remote Services, Physical Media, Data Interception

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The threat actor utilizes cross-site identification techniques delivered via spearphishing emails specifically targeting energy sector personnel.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-30: Hijacking a Privileged Thread of Execution
stage.description: Upon successful delivery of the spearphishing link, the Mango backdoor leverages OS API calls to hijack a legitimate process thread for execution.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The Mango backdoor establishes persistence by manipulating key configuration files or scheduled tasks on compromised endpoints.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: This stage represents the ongoing unauthorized activity enabled by the Mango backdoor presence, potentially collecting sensitive information relevant to the energy sector operations.

Scenario.summary: Simulated a simple targeted attack against the Energy sector involving spearphishing for initial access, execution via thread hijacking, persistence established through endpoint manipulation, and subsequent unauthorized activity monitoring.
